hero.title: Cultural Bridges
hero.subtitle: Strengthening Interculturality and Social Inclusion among Youth
hero.quote: Building bridges between cultures, breaking walls between people.

project.title: The Project
project.desc1: Cultural Bridges (CCIY) is an Erasmus+ KA210-YOU partnership that brings together three youth organisations from Spain, Poland, and France to foster intercultural understanding, fight discrimination, and promote social inclusion among young Europeans.
project.desc2: Over 18 months (December 2024 - May 2026), the project creates spaces for youth from diverse backgrounds to learn from each other, challenge stereotypes, and build lasting connections across borders.

approach.title: Our Approach
approach.pillar1_desc: Interactive workshops, peer learning, and creative expression to build intercultural competences outside the classroom.
approach.pillar2_desc: Direct exchange experiences across three countries, bringing young people face to face with different cultures, traditions, and perspectives.
approach.pillar3_desc: Equipping participants with the skills, confidence, and networks to become active agents of inclusion in their own communities.

cta.title: Discover our deliverables
cta.desc: Activity reports, pedagogical guides, brochures and more.

blog_page.title: Blog & Articles
blog_page.subtitle: Press releases, testimonials and opinion pieces from the Cultural Bridges project

results_page.title: Results & Deliverables
results_page.subtitle: All documents and outputs from the Cultural Bridges project
results_page.note: All documents are available in PDF format. For more information, contact one of the project partners.

about_page.title: About the project
about_page.subtitle: Cultural Bridges: Strengthening Interculturality and Social Inclusion among Youth
about_page.challenge_p1: Across Europe, young people continue to face barriers rooted in racism, discrimination, and social exclusion. Migrant and minority youth are disproportionately affected.
about_page.challenge_p2: Cultural Bridges was designed in response to this reality -- because inclusion begins with encounter.
about_page.obj1_desc: Enable participants to move beyond stereotypes and develop a deeper appreciation of cultural differences and similarities.
about_page.obj2_desc: Give young people the opportunity to practice communication, teamwork, and conflict resolution in real-life, multicultural settings.
about_page.obj3_title: Build an international network of engaged youth
about_page.obj3_desc: Foster the creation of lasting relationships and joint projects that promote inclusion and diversity across borders.
about_page.disclaimer: This project has been funded with support from the European Commission. This publication reflects the views only of the authors.

about_partners.org1_desc: Youth association based in Lleida working for social inclusion and empowerment of young people from diverse backgrounds through non-formal education and European projects.
about_partners.org2_desc: Polish youth organisation based in Warsaw, committed to promoting intercultural dialogue, fighting discrimination, and integrating young migrants.
about_partners.org3_desc: French association located in Ile-de-France working for the social inclusion of young people through civic, educational, and intercultural actions.

timeline.t1_desc: Official start of the partnership and activity planning.
timeline.t2_desc: Preparatory workshops in each partner country with young participants.
timeline.t3_desc: 5 days of cultural exchanges and field visits with 30 participants in Warsaw, Poland.
timeline.t4_desc: Publication of articles, social media content, and community events in all 3 countries.
timeline.t5_desc: Final evaluation, sustainability plan, and report submission to the national agency.

footer.tagline: Strengthening Interculturality and Social Inclusion among Youth
footer.programme_desc: Co-funded by the European Union through the Erasmus+ programme. Views expressed are those of the authors only.

articles.a1_title: 30 Young Europeans Unite in Warsaw to Build Cultural Bridges
articles.a1_excerpt: Thirty young people from three European countries gathered in Warsaw from May 14 to 18, 2025, for a landmark youth exchange under the Erasmus+ project Cultural Bridges.
articles.a2_title: What I Learned About Myself by Living with 29 Strangers from 3 Countries
articles.a2_excerpt: I boarded the plane to Warsaw terrified. What I did not expect was that it would change the way I see myself.
articles.a3_title: Why Non-Formal Education Is the Key to Fighting Racism Among Youth
articles.a3_excerpt: Europe has a racism problem, and traditional education alone is not solving it. At the Cultural Bridges project, we believe the answer lies in non-formal education.

eu_disclaimer: Co-funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are however those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the European Union or the EACEA.
